title: Government of Malaysia

infobox.value: [Yang di-Pertuan Agong](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Agong "Yang di-Pertuan Agong")   [Ibrahim Iskandar of Johor](/wiki/Ibrahim_Iskandar_of_Johor "Ibrahim Iskandar of Johor")

([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Kerajaan Persekutuan Malaysia*; [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): ‏كراجأن ڤرسكوتوان مليسيا‎), is based in the [Federal Territory](/wiki/Federal_Territories_of_Malaysia "Federal Territories of Malaysia") of [Putrajaya](/wiki/Putrajaya "Putrajaya"), with the exception of the legislative branch, which is located in [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur"). [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") is a federation composed of [the 11 States of Malaya, the Borneo States of Sabah and Sarawak, and 3 Federal Territories](/wiki/States_and_federal_territories_of_Malaysia "States and federal territories of Malaysia") operating within a [constitutional monarchy](/wiki/Constitutional_monarchy "Constitutional monarchy") under the [Westminster system](/wiki/Westminster_system "Westminster system") and is categorised as a [representative democracy](/wiki/Representative_democracy "Representative democracy"). The [federal government](/wiki/Federal_government "Federal government") of [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") adheres to and is created by the [Federal Constitution of Malaysia](/wiki/Constitution_of_Malaysia "Constitution of Malaysia"), the supreme law of the land.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Federal Government of Malaysia   
 *Kerajaan Persekutuan Malaysia*   
 ‏كراجأن ڤرسكوتوان مليسيا‎

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [Ensign of the Government of Malaysia](/wiki/File:Coat_of_arms_of_Malaysia.svg "Ensign of the Government of Malaysia") [Coat of arms of Malaysia](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Malaysia "Coat of arms of Malaysia") | |
| Formation | 16 September 1963; 62 years ago (1963-09-16)[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Country | [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") |
| Website | [www.malaysia.gov.my](https://www.malaysia.gov.my/portal/index) |
| Legislative branch | |
| Legislature | [Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Malaysia "Parliament of Malaysia") |
| Meeting place | [Houses of Parliament](/wiki/Malaysian_Houses_of_Parliament "Malaysian Houses of Parliament") |
| Executive branch | |
| Leader | [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Malaysia "Prime Minister of Malaysia")   [Anwar Ibrahim](/wiki/Anwar_Ibrahim "Anwar Ibrahim") |
| Appointer | [Yang di-Pertuan Agong](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Agong "Yang di-Pertuan Agong")   [Ibrahim Iskandar of Johor](/wiki/Ibrahim_Iskandar_of_Johor "Ibrahim Iskandar of Johor") |
| Headquarters | [Perdana Putra](/wiki/Perdana_Putra "Perdana Putra") |
| Main organ | [Cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Malaysia "Cabinet of Malaysia") |
| Judicial branch | |
| Court | [Federal Court](/wiki/Federal_Court_of_Malaysia "Federal Court of Malaysia") |
| Seat | [Palace of Justice](/wiki/Palace_of_Justice_(Malaysia) "Palace of Justice (Malaysia)") |

The **Government of Malaysia**, officially the **Federal Government of Malaysia** ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Kerajaan Persekutuan Malaysia*; [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_script "Jawi script"): ‏كراجأن ڤرسكوتوان مليسيا‎), is based in the [Federal Territory](/wiki/Federal_Territories_of_Malaysia "Federal Territories of Malaysia") of [Putrajaya](/wiki/Putrajaya "Putrajaya"), with the exception of the legislative branch, which is located in [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur"). [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") is a federation composed of [the 11 States of Malaya, the Borneo States of Sabah and Sarawak, and 3 Federal Territories](/wiki/States_and_federal_territories_of_Malaysia "States and federal territories of Malaysia") operating within a [constitutional monarchy](/wiki/Constitutional_monarchy "Constitutional monarchy") under the [Westminster system](/wiki/Westminster_system "Westminster system") and is categorised as a [representative democracy](/wiki/Representative_democracy "Representative democracy"). The [federal government](/wiki/Federal_government "Federal government") of [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia") adheres to and is created by the [Federal Constitution of Malaysia](/wiki/Constitution_of_Malaysia "Constitution of Malaysia"), the supreme law of the land.

The federal government adopts the principle of [separation of powers](/wiki/Separation_of_powers "Separation of powers") under Article 127 of the Federal Constitution of Malaysia,[[2]](#cite_note-2) and has three branches: the [executive](/wiki/Executive_(government) "Executive (government)"), [legislature](/wiki/Legislature "Legislature"), and [judiciary](/wiki/Judiciary "Judiciary").[[3]](#cite_note-3) The [state governments](/wiki/State_governments_of_Malaysia "State governments of Malaysia") in Malaysia also have their respective executive and legislative bodies. The judicial system in Malaysia is a federalised court system operating uniformly throughout the country.

## Federal government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Federal government")]

See also: [Federalism in Malaysia](/wiki/Federalism_in_Malaysia "Federalism in Malaysia")

The federal or [central government](/wiki/Central_government "Central government") is the ultimate authority in Malaysia and located in [Putrajaya](/wiki/Putrajaya "Putrajaya"). It is headed by the [Prime Minister of Malaysia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Malaysia "Prime Minister of Malaysia") as the [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government").

### Legislature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Legislature")]

Main article: [Parliament of Malaysia](/wiki/Parliament_of_Malaysia "Parliament of Malaysia")

The bicameral parliament consists of the [lower house](/wiki/Lower_house "Lower house"), the House of Representatives or *[Dewan Rakyat](/wiki/Dewan_Rakyat "Dewan Rakyat")* (literally the "Chamber of the People") and the [upper house](/wiki/Upper_house "Upper house"), the Senate or *[Dewan Negara](/wiki/Dewan_Negara "Dewan Negara")* (literally the "Chamber of the Nation").[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-autogenerated1-5) All seventy [Senate](/wiki/Senate "Senate") members sit for three-year terms (to a maximum of two terms); twenty-six are elected by the thirteen state assemblies, and forty-four are appointed by the [Yang di-Pertuan Agong](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Agong "Yang di-Pertuan Agong") (King of Malaysia) on the advice of the Prime Minister.[[6]](#cite_note-state.gov-6) The 222 members of the *Dewan Rakyat* are elected from single-member districts by [universal suffrage](/wiki/Universal_suffrage "Universal suffrage"). The parliament follows a [multi-party system](/wiki/Multi-party_system "Multi-party system") and the governing body is elected through [first-past-the-post voting](/wiki/First-past-the-post_voting "First-past-the-post voting"). [Parliament](/wiki/Parliament_of_Malaysia "Parliament of Malaysia") has a maximum mandate of five years by law. The Yang di-Pertuan Agong may dissolve parliament at any time and usually does so upon the advice of the Prime Minister.

### Executive

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Executive")]

Main article: [Cabinet of Malaysia](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Malaysia "Cabinet of Malaysia")

While the monarch is the [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state"), real executive power is vested in the Cabinet, led by the [prime minister](/wiki/Prime_minister "Prime minister") as the [head of government](/wiki/Head_of_government "Head of government"); the Malaysian constitution stipulates that the prime minister must be a member of the [lower house](/wiki/Lower_house "Lower house") of [parliament](/wiki/Parliament "Parliament") who, in the opinion of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, commands a [majority](/wiki/Majority "Majority") in parliament.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The Cabinet is chosen from among members of both houses of Parliament and is responsible to that body.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The Executive branch of the Government of Malaysia consists of the [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Malaysia "Prime Minister of Malaysia") as the head of the government, followed by the various ministers of the [Cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Malaysia "Cabinet of Malaysia").

### Judiciary

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Judiciary")]

Main article: [Judiciary of Malaysia](/wiki/Judiciary_of_Malaysia "Judiciary of Malaysia")

The highest court in the judicial system is the [Federal Court](/wiki/Federal_Court_of_Malaysia "Federal Court of Malaysia"), followed by the [Court of Appeal](/wiki/Court_of_Appeal_of_Malaysia "Court of Appeal of Malaysia"), and two [High Courts](/wiki/High_court_(Malaysia) "High court (Malaysia)"), one for [Peninsular Malaysia](/wiki/Peninsular_Malaysia "Peninsular Malaysia"), and one for [East Malaysia](/wiki/East_Malaysia "East Malaysia"). The subordinate courts in each of these jurisdictions include [Sessions Courts](/wiki/Sessions_Court "Sessions Court"), [Magistrates' Courts](/wiki/Magistrates%27_court "Magistrates' court"), and [Courts for Children](/wiki/Juvenile_court "Juvenile court"). Malaysia also has a Special Court to hear cases brought by or against all Royalty.

## Head of state

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Head of state")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/de/Kuala_Lumpur_Malaysia-Istana_Negara-Jalan-Duta-01.jpg/250px-Kuala_Lumpur_Malaysia-Istana_Negara-Jalan-Duta-01.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kuala_Lumpur_Malaysia-Istana_Negara-Jalan-Duta-01.jpg)

The [Istana Negara](/wiki/Istana_Negara,_Jalan_Tuanku_Abdul_Halim "Istana Negara, Jalan Tuanku Abdul Halim"), the residence and office of the [Yang di-Pertuan Agong](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Agong "Yang di-Pertuan Agong").

The [Yang di-Pertuan Agong](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertuan_Agong "Yang di-Pertuan Agong") (lit. 'He Who is Made Lord',[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Jawi](/wiki/Jawi_alphabet "Jawi alphabet"): يڠ دڤرتوان اݢوڠ), also known as the Supreme Head or the King, is the [constitutional monarch](/wiki/Constitutional_monarch "Constitutional monarch") and [head of state](/wiki/Head_of_state "Head of state") of [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"). The office was established in 1957, when the [Federation of Malaya](/wiki/Federation_of_Malaya "Federation of Malaya") (now Malaysia) gained [independence](/wiki/Independence_of_Malaya "Independence of Malaya") from the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). The Yang di-Pertuan Agong is elected by the [Conference of Rulers](/wiki/Conference_of_Rulers "Conference of Rulers"), comprising the nine [rulers of the Malay states](/wiki/Monarchies_of_Malaysia "Monarchies of Malaysia"), with the office *[de facto](/wiki/De_facto "De facto")* rotated between them, making Malaysia one of the world's few [elective monarchies](/wiki/Elective_monarchies "Elective monarchies"). In accordance with Article 41 of the Constitution, the *Yang di-Pertuan Agong* is Commander-in-Chief of the [Malaysian Armed Forces](/wiki/Malaysian_Armed_Forces "Malaysian Armed Forces"). As such, he is the highest-ranking officer in the [military chain of command](/wiki/Military_chain_of_command "Military chain of command").

## Head of government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Head of government")]

[![Perdana Putra.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a9/Perdana_Putra_building_2005.jpg/250px-Perdana_Putra_building_2005.jpg)](/wiki/File:Perdana_Putra_building_2005.jpg)

[Perdana Putra](/wiki/Perdana_Putra "Perdana Putra") houses the office complex of the [Prime Minister of Malaysia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Malaysia "Prime Minister of Malaysia").

The [Prime Minister of Malaysia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Malaysia "Prime Minister of Malaysia") ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Perdana Menteri Malaysia*) is the indirect head of government (executive) of Malaysia. The prime minister is appointed by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, the head of state, and is someone who in the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's opinion is likely to command the confidence of the majority of the members of that House of Representatives (*Dewan Rakyat*), the elected lower house of Parliament. The prime minister heads the [Cabinet](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Malaysia "Cabinet of Malaysia"), whose members are appointed by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, on the advice of the prime minister. The prime minister and his Cabinet are collectively responsible to Parliament. The [Prime Minister's Department](/wiki/Prime_Minister%27s_Department_(Malaysia) "Prime Minister's Department (Malaysia)") (sometimes referred to as the Prime Minister's Office) is the body and ministry in which the prime minister exercises their functions and powers.

## State governments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: State governments")]

Main articles: [State governments of Malaysia](/wiki/State_governments_of_Malaysia "State governments of Malaysia") and [State legislative assemblies of Malaysia](/wiki/State_legislative_assemblies_of_Malaysia "State legislative assemblies of Malaysia")

Each state government in Malaysia is created by its respective state constitution. Each state has a unicameral [state legislative chamber](/wiki/Dewan_Undangan_Negeri "Dewan Undangan Negeri") (Malay: *Dewan Undangan Negeri*) whose members are elected from single-member [constituencies](/wiki/List_of_Malaysian_electoral_districts "List of Malaysian electoral districts"). State executive councils (EXCO) of states of Malaya, the [Cabinet of Sabah](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Sabah "Cabinet of Sabah") and the [Cabinet of Sarawak](/wiki/Cabinet_of_Sarawak "Cabinet of Sarawak") are led by [Menteri Besar](/wiki/Menteri_besar "Menteri besar") or [Chief Ministers](/wiki/Chief_ministers_in_Malaysia "Chief ministers in Malaysia") in states without hereditary rulers,[[6]](#cite_note-state.gov-6) who are state assembly members from the majority party in the state legislative chamber. They advise their respective [sultans](/wiki/Sultan#Current_sultans "Sultan") or [governors](/wiki/Yang_di-Pertua_Negeri "Yang di-Pertua Negeri"). In each of the states with a hereditary ruler, the *Menteri Besar* is required to be a [Malay](/wiki/Malays_(ethnic_group) "Malays (ethnic group)"), appointed by the sultan.

## Local governments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Local governments")]

Main article: [Local government in Malaysia](/wiki/Local_government_in_Malaysia "Local government in Malaysia")

The local government or local authority (Malay: *kerajaan tempatan* or *pihak berkuasa tempatan* (PBT)) is the lowest level in the system of government in Malaysia—after federal and state. It has the power to collect taxes (in the form of [assessment tax](/wiki/Assessment_tax "Assessment tax")), to create laws and rules (in the form of by-laws) and to grant licenses and permits for any trade in its area of jurisdiction, in addition to providing [basic amenities](/wiki/Amenity "Amenity"), collecting and managing waste and garbage as well as planning and developing the area under its jurisdiction. Local authorities in Malaysia are generally under the exclusive purview of the state governments and headed by a civil servant with the title Yang Di-Pertua (President). Local government areas and the boundaries are usually consistent with [district](/wiki/District "District") boundaries but there are some places where the boundaries are not consistent and may overlap with adjoining districts especially in urbanised areas.

Unlike the federal and state governments, the local governments in Malaysia are not elected but appointed by the state government after local council elections were suspended by the federal government in 1965.

## Military, police and other governmental bodies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Military, police and other governmental bodies")]

The [Malaysian Armed Forces](/wiki/Malaysian_Armed_Forces "Malaysian Armed Forces") are the [military body](/wiki/Military "Military") of Malaysia while the [Royal Malaysia Police](/wiki/Royal_Malaysian_Police "Royal Malaysian Police") are in charge of [law enforcement](/wiki/Law_enforcement "Law enforcement").

## Legal system

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Legal system")]

Main article: [Law of Malaysia](/wiki/Law_of_Malaysia "Law of Malaysia")

The law of Malaysia is mainly based on the common law [legal system](/wiki/Legal_system "Legal system"). This was a direct result of the colonisation of Malaya, [Sarawak](/wiki/Sarawak "Sarawak"), and [North Borneo](/wiki/North_Borneo "North Borneo") by [Britain](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") between the early 19th century to 1960s. The supreme law of the land—the Constitution of Malaysia—sets out the legal framework and rights of [Malaysian](/wiki/Malaysians "Malaysians") citizens. Federal laws enacted by the Parliament of Malaysia apply throughout the country. There are also state laws enacted by the State Legislative Assemblies which applies in the particular state. The constitution of Malaysia also provides for a unique dual justice system—the secular laws (criminal and civil) and [sharia laws](/wiki/Sharia "Sharia").

Articles 73 to 79 of the Federal Constitution specifies the subject in which the federal and state government may legislate. Parliament has the exclusive power to make laws over matters falling under the Federal List (such as citizenship, defence, internal security, civil and criminal law, finance, trade, commerce and industry, education, labour, and tourism) whereas each state, through its Legislative Assembly, has legislative power over matters under the State List (such as land, local government, sharia law and sharia courts, state holidays and state public works). Parliament and state legislatures share the power to make laws over matters under the Concurrent List (such as water supplies and housing) but Article 75 provides that in the event of conflict, federal law will prevail over state law.

## Elections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Government_of_Malaysia&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Elections")]

Main article: [Elections in Malaysia](/wiki/Elections_in_Malaysia "Elections in Malaysia")

Elections in Malaysia exist at two levels: national level and state level. National level elections are those for membership in the *Dewan Rakyat*, the lower house of Parliament, while state level elections are for membership in the various State Legislative Assemblies. The head of the executive branch, the prime minister, is appointed from among members of the winning coalition.
